title: JWST Detects Water Vapor in Super-Earth Atmosphere
summary: Astronomers used Webb spectroscopy to identify water vapor signatures in the atmosphere of exoplanet K2-18 c.
body: A multinational astronomy team reported high-confidence detection of water vapor bands while observing transits of K2-18 c. The analysis combined near-infrared spectra over multiple observing windows and corrected for stellar activity. Researchers say the finding improves confidence that rocky super-Earth atmospheres can be chemically profiled at long distances. The team cautions that water vapor does not indicate habitability by itself and additional compounds must be measured.

title: CRISPR Therapy Restores Vision in Early Human Trial
summary: A phase 1/2 trial reported measurable visual improvement in participants with inherited retinal disease.
body: Clinical researchers announced interim data from a CRISPR-based therapy designed to edit a retinal gene linked to progressive blindness. Participants received localized treatment and were evaluated using visual acuity, low-light navigation, and retinal imaging. Investigators observed functional gains in several participants with no serious treatment-related adverse events reported so far. The team emphasized that larger studies are required to confirm long-term efficacy and safety.

title: New Solid-State Battery Prototype Doubles Fast-Charge Cycles
summary: Materials scientists demonstrated a sulfide-based electrolyte design that sustained rapid charging across 1,500 cycles.
body: Engineers at a university-industry lab unveiled a prototype solid-state battery cell with improved interface stability during high-rate charging. By tuning the electrolyte composition and stack pressure, the team reduced dendrite growth and thermal hotspots. The prototype retained over 82% capacity after 1,500 rapid cycles in controlled testing. Researchers say scale-up and manufacturing compatibility remain major next steps before commercial deployment.
